description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a green and tan camouflage paint scheme, featuring two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked one above the other and supported by a network of struts. The fuselage is streamlined and tapers toward the tail, with a single vertical stabilizer and a horizontal tailplane. The front of the airplane is equipped with two prominent piston engines mounted between the wings, each with a wooden propeller. The cockpit is positioned at the front of the fuselage and appears to be open, typical of early 20th-century aircraft. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tailwheel, arranged in a taildragger configuration. The overall design suggests a classic, early military or stunt aircraft, likely constructed from a combination of metal framing and fabric-covered surfaces.
description: A vintage green and tan biplane with dual piston engines, open cockpit, and taildragger landing gear, featuring stacked wings and a streamlined fuselage.
description_view_0: Side-rear view showing the tapering fuselage, single vertical stabilizer, and the stacked biplane wings with visible struts.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the twin engines, dual propellers, and the full span of the upper and lower wings.
description_view_2: Side-rear view from the opposite side, displaying the tail section, wing structure, and engine placement.
description_view_3: Front view showing the open cockpit, twin engines with propellers, and the symmetrical biplane wing arrangement.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and supported by interplane struts; no winglets or noticeable dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin piston engines mounted between the upper and lower wings, each with a wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal tailplane.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two main fixed wheels and a small tailwheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open cockpit located at the front of the fuselage, likely single-seat.
